DiffeoForge modern objective benchmark

Project: synthetic-modern-atlas-blockwise
Config: minimal-modern-atlas-blockwise.yaml
Created: 2026-07-22T16:58:00+00:00

Interpretation boundary: Fresh CPU processes measure one explicitly declared exact objective plus gradient for the first configured optimizer block. Wall time excludes tensor/input preparation and warm-up. Process RSS is sampled every 5 ms after warm-up and can miss shorter peaks; it is not total system memory, GPU memory, full-optimizer peak RAM, end-to-end workflow runtime, an ETA model, an extrapolation to 300 subjects, or a hardware pass/fail verdict.

Measured protocol

  • Subjects: 5 of 5
  • Gradient block: momenta
  • Warm-ups per repeat: 1
  • Fresh-process repeats: 5
  • Threads: 1
  • Source-declared pairwise execution: blockwise (64 × 64)
  • Effective benchmark-only pairwise execution: blockwise (32 × 32)
  • Tile autograd strategy: recompute
  • RSS sampling interval: 5.000 ms
  • Logical Gaussian pair elements/evaluation: 1,606,065
  • Largest dense-equivalent execution XYZ payload: 0.02 MiB

Descriptive summary

  • Median measured wall time: 779.739 ms
  • Median sampled peak RSS: 299.81 MiB
  • Median sampled RSS delta after warm-up: 10.09 MiB
  • Numerically consistent within declared tolerance: true

Per-repeat observations

RepeatWall msRSS before Sampled peak RSSSampled deltaObjective Gradient norm
1779.739289.49 MiB299.59 MiB10.09 MiB-44.0713892167233.048176826
2813.378289.54 MiB299.81 MiB10.27 MiB-44.0713892167233.048176826
3764.620290.06 MiB299.97 MiB9.91 MiB-44.0713892167233.048176826
4764.543289.19 MiB299.91 MiB10.72 MiB-44.0713892167233.048176826
5808.149289.82 MiB299.51 MiB9.69 MiB-44.0713892167233.048176826

Warnings

  • Sampled process RSS can miss peaks shorter than the 5 ms sampling interval.
  • This measures one objective+gradient evaluation, not a full optimizer or workflow.
  • Do not extrapolate these repeats to 300 subjects without a prospective scaling design.
  • Recompute trades additional backward calculation for a different saved-tensor graph.

Machine-readable values are in benchmark.json; raw repeat rows are in samples.csv.
